key_insight: An ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm can discover truly novel crystal structures by exploring complex energy landscapes beyond database-trained methods.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable prediction of stable and metastable phases for designing new quantum and magnetic materials from composition alone.
method: Combines an ANN-based interatomic potential with an iterative genetic algorithm.
Adds a metadynamics-inspired penalty to avoid revisiting known minima and promote broader structure search.
Targets both global minima and competing metastable phases in complex ternary systems.
evidence_signal: The method correctly predicted the Pbam ground-state structure of La4Co4Pb and the orthorhombic structure of La5CoPb2, matching independent x-ray diffraction results.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the ML potential and the ability of the search strategy to sufficiently cover very rugged, high-dimensional composition spaces.
paper_title: Complex crystal structure prediction using ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm
